action: Open Google Chrome on the computer. After Chrome opens, type "youtube" in the search bar.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open Google Chrome and then type 'youtube' in the search bar. On the current screen, I can see the Google Chrome web browser icon (Box ID 100). The first step is to open Chrome by clicking on its icon.

previous action: I was instructed to open Google Chrome by clicking its icon (Box ID 100). The current screen shows a Chrome window open with the address bar visible at the top. This confirms that Chrome has launched successfully. The next step is to type 'youtube' in the search bar (address bar) at the top of the Chrome window. Since the address bar is already focused (as indicated by the blinking cursor), I can proceed to type 'youtube'.

previous action: I was instructed to type 'youtube' in the Chrome address bar. Looking at the current screen, I can see that 'youtube' is now present in the search/address bar at the top of the Chrome window. This confirms that the previous action was successful. The next logical step is to execute the search by pressing 'Enter' so that Chrome will search for 'youtube'.

previous action: I was instructed to press 'Enter' after typing 'youtube' in the Chrome address bar. The current screen shows Google search results for 'youtube', confirming that the search was executed successfully. The next step, based on the original task, would be to open YouTube. The first search result is the official YouTube link (Box ID 17, 'YouTube' with URL https://www.youtube.com). The logical next action is to click on this first search result to open YouTube.
